page: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network-based resources.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and multi-core processing. The cached read-only memory bandwidth reaches 7016.18 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are 229.99 GB/sec and 94.79 GB/sec respectively. In computational tests, the server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 88,827.32 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,623.0, both placing in the top tier. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) contributes to strong performance in specialized tasks, such as a PassMark encryption test score of 90,730.06 and LLM inference prompt processing speeds of 294.37 tokens/sec for a 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.metal-24xl offers high resource density for memory-bound applications without the overhead of virtualization, though the lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage provisioning. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 38 Gbps. This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and software compilation tasks, as evidenced by its strong database operations benchmark of 31,503.18 and top-tier clang compilation score of 59,290.0.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs on 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores with a 3.2 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities, including a PassMark CPU score of 88,827.32 and cached memory read bandwidth of 7,016.18 GB/sec. Advanced instruction support, such as AVX-512 and AMX, enables strong cryptographic and vector processing. This bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors with AVX-512 and AMX support
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of 7016.18 GB/sec (cached)
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage bundled
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and software compilation
